vinyl_cutter.description: The Vinyl Cutter is a core piece of lab equipment used for educational and prototyping purposes.
vinyl_cutter.overview: This tool allows students to interact with fundamental principles of engineering. It is heavily utilized in the prototyping_lab.
vinyl_cutter.working_principle: Operates on standard engineering principles specific to its domain. Converts inputs into measured or fabricated outputs.
vinyl_cutter.historical_background: Early versions of the Vinyl Cutter revolutionized engineering workflows.
interesting_facts: Often the first machine students learn in their respective domains.
vinyl_cutter.safety_summary: Always wear appropriate PPE. Follow lab protocols. Do not operate unsupervised if required.
vinyl_cutter.sop_summary: Power on, initialize/calibrate, perform operation, clean up, power down.
vinyl_cutter.maintenance_notes: Regularly clean the workspace and verify calibration standards.
vinyl_cutter.calibration_notes: Calibrate before critical measurements or high-precision fabrication.

project_examples.name: Introductory Lab
project_examples.description: A simple guided exercise to learn the Vinyl Cutter.

project_examples.name: Intermediate Project
project_examples.description: Applying multiple concepts together.

project_examples.name: Capstone Module
project_examples.description: Full integration into a larger system.
